⭐ NOK -2.8% · NOK is down 2.77% today on…

Roku going under a single content owner (Fox) creates conflicts for advertisers wanting neutral CTV measurement/buying; The Trade Desk's independent DSP and OpenPath become more strategically valuable
Magnite is Roku's SSP partner and a pure-play CTV SSP — deal validates CTV platform value and Magnite re-rates as the next scarce independent CTV ad-tech asset; possible takeout target itself.

Disney loses a neutral, scaled distribution partner — Hulu/Disney+ now distributed on a platform owned by direct rival Fox, weakening negotiating leverage on carriage economics and ad inventory splits
Max distribution on Roku now runs through a competitor; WBD's already weak streaming scale position worsens versus a combined Fox+Roku ad-supported powerhouse.
Connected-TV pure-play with sports tilt — re-rates on CTV M&A multiple read-through (Fox paid up for streaming distribution); also a potential consolidation candidate.
A Fox-owned Roku could deprioritize Netflix tiles/recommendations on the #1 US streaming OS, modestly pressuring sub-acquisition efficiency on that surface.
Counter-risk: Roku's first-party ad inventory and ACR data may get pulled in-house to Fox's ad stack, reducing programmatic supply available through independent DSPs.
Comcast/NBCU's Peacock loses leverage on the largest US connected-TV OS now controlled by a content rival; also raises antitrust attention on its own potential streaming M&A path.
⭐ NOK +4.8% · NOK is up 4.82% today with no…
🎯 RIVN +7.9% · Rivian jumped nearly 8% after news of…
Intuitive Machines benefits from space-sector flow and is a SpaceX launch customer; sector multiple expansion plus customer-validation read-through.
Boeing's ULA JV and Starliner program lose further mindshare and pricing power as a publicly-valued SpaceX cements launch dominance and crew/cargo wins.
Iridium is the established satellite-comms incumbent; gets pulled along on space-basket flows even though Starlink is a competitor — net positive on sector re-rating short term.
Lockheed (other half of ULA) faces ongoing share loss in national security launch to a now-public, well-capitalized SpaceX able to fund Starship ramp.
Viasat's consumer/aviation broadband business is structurally pressured by a Starlink that now has fresh equity-market currency to accelerate consumer rollout.
Closest pure-play public launch comp to SpaceX (Neutron vs Falcon 9); SpaceX's premium IPO multiple forces re-rating of Rocket Lab's launch + space systems business.
Direct-to-cell satellite broadband peer/competitor to Starlink; SpaceX's public valuation gives investors a yardstick that lifts ASTS's constellation NPV math.
Planet Labs is a pure-play satellite data name that re-rates with the space sector basket as SpaceX IPO draws generalist capital into listed space comps.
Redwire is a small-cap space infrastructure supplier that sells hardware into SpaceX/NASA ecosystem; benefits from SpaceX-driven capex narrative and basket bid.

Mercury Systems supplies rad-hard processing and RF subsystems used in space/defense satellite platforms; benefits from validated space-economy capex narrative post-SpaceX IPO.
Iridium is a satellite operator with optical/RF supply ties; rides the satellite-economy halo and laser-comm narrative without being a direct Starlink competitor.
AeroVironment (post-BlueHalo) has space/space-domain awareness exposure and rides the broader defense-space capex wave amplified by SpaceX's public-market validation.
Note: also faces some Starlink competitive pressure in IoT/L-band — net direction uncertain, listed as beneficiary above but worth flagging two-sided risk.
Intuitive Machines competes for NASA lunar/transport dollars where SpaceX's Starship is the dominant alternative; SpaceX IPO strengthens that competitive overhang.

Lumentum is a direct photonics peer to COHR with exposure to optical communications/laser components used in satellite inter-satellite links; re-rates on the same Starlink laser-comm read-through driv
Rocket Lab is the most direct listed competitor to SpaceX in small-launch and now medium-launch (Neutron); capital and investor mindshare rotate to the SpaceX vehicle.
AST SpaceMobile competes directly with Starlink Direct-to-Cell; SpaceX's public valuation puts a competitive ceiling/funding overhang on ASTS narrative.
Viasat's GEO broadband business is structurally disadvantaged vs. a now-public, well-capitalized Starlink; re-rates lower on competitive read-through.

⭐ ORCL -8.7% · Oracle dropped sharply after the…
⭐ RKLB +8.4% · RKLB surged 8.4% alongside a broader…
Broadcom supplies networking silicon (Tomahawk/Jericho) and custom ASIC capability used in hyperscale AI fabrics; Oracle's buildout expands the addressable networking spend.
Arista's 800G Ethernet switches are the backbone of AI training clusters; Oracle scaling OCI capacity raises switch port demand across the hyperscale tier.
Digital Realty leases data center capacity to Oracle and benefits from spillover demand when hyperscalers can't self-build fast enough; Oracle's capex magnitude tightens overall DC supply.
Similar capex-margin compression read-through; if Oracle is spending this aggressively to keep up, GCP faces the same arms-race pressure on AI infra spend vs. revenue conversion.
Enterprise hybrid cloud competitor — Oracle's willingness to spend at hyperscale levels widens the capability gap for IBM's smaller cloud/AI infrastructure footprint.
Oracle's elevated FY27 capex is overwhelmingly GPU-bound; NVDA is the primary recipient of OCI's H100/H200/Blackwell orders. Higher Oracle capex = higher NVDA backlog visibility.

Oracle's aggressive OCI capex signals intensifying hyperscale competition for AI workloads and raises the bar on Azure capex too — same margin-pressure narrative applies, plus competitive read-through
Vertiv is a key supplier of power and liquid cooling infrastructure for OCI data center buildouts; capex shock directly translates to order flow for thermal/power management gear.
GE Vernova's gas turbines and grid equipment are bottleneck items for new AI data centers; Oracle's capex scale reinforces the power generation buildout thesis.
